overview: This venomous snake is identified as the White-banded Coralsnake (Micrurus albicinctus). It is a small, elapid snake typically found in South America, particularly in Brazil and Colombia. The species is listed as "Least Concern" regarding its conservation status. It is known to be nocturnal and semi-burrowing, making encounters with humans rare. iNaturalist +4 5 sites White-banded Coralsnake (Micrurus albicinctus) · iNaturalist
